Title: Genome- and transcriptome-wide association studies reveal the genetic basis and the breeding history of seed glucosinolate content in Brassica napus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates seed glucosinolate accumulation; loss of function reduces seed glucosinolate content but alters seed development traits, increases seed oil content, decreases seed size/TSW, decreases protein content, decreases leaf glucosinolates, and increases root glucosinolates.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; TWAS; WGCNA; POCKET prioritization; haplotype analysis; expression–phenotype correlation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling; RNA-seq differential expression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stages at 20 DAF and 40 DAF; field-grown in Wuhan across 2017–2019
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC02.GTR2 was prioritized as the candidate causal gene underlying qGSL.C02.1 by integrating GWAS, TWAS, co-expression, and POCKET analyses. 2. Its expression was positively correlated with seed glucosinolate content at both 20 and 40 DAF. 3. CRISPR-derived gtr2 mutants demonstrated that BnaC02.GTR2 and paralogs promote seed glucosinolate accumulation. 4. BnaC02.GTR2 disruption caused pleiotropic effects on seed development, including smaller seeds, reduced thousand seed weight, altered fatty acid composition, reduced protein content, and increased seed oil content. 5. In vegetative tissues, BnaC02.gtr2 decreased leaf glucosinolates but increased root glucosinolates, consistent with a transport role.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRT/PTR family transporter with MFS conserved domain
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HAG1/MYB28; BAT5; CYP83A1; AOP2; GTR3 (transcript-level association)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively associated with seed glucosinolate content at 20 and 40 DAF developing seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural low-SGC haplotypes at BnaC02.GTR2 are valuable for breeding reduced seed glucosinolate without significant SOC/TSW penalty in natural accessions; complete knockout can strongly lower seed glucosinolate but causes adverse seed-trait pleiotropy, so partial/precise editing is recommended.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate underlying qGSL.C02.1; four major haplotypes detected in coding plus promoter region, with haplotype B associated with significantly higher seed glucosinolate. Natural favorable haplotypes appear partially functional and differ from complete CRISPR knockout.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe reduction in thousand seed weight and seed size in multi-gtr2 mutants; altered fatty acid composition; reduced protein content; possible broad perturbation of seed development.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of 505 accessions; seed RNA-seq/TWAS at 20 and 40 DAF; mutant seed RNA-seq at 20 DAF

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates seed glucosinolate accumulation; knockout reduces seed glucosinolate, with stronger effect among tested paralogs and additive effects in combined mutants.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL co-localization; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA02.GTR2 was located in qGSL.A02.2 and inferred as a major paralog contributing to seed glucosinolate accumulation. 2. Single mutant significantly reduced seed glucosinolate, and double/triple/quadruple mutants further reduced levels, indicating partial redundancy. 3. Natural low-SGC haplotypes at the GTR2-associated QTLs contributed to breeding improvement.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRT/PTR family transporter; major facilitator superfamily conserved domain
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for stacking with other low-SGC loci, but complete knockout may carry seed-trait penalties.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Present in qGSL.A02.2; region showed low nucleotide diversity and conservation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to reduced seed size/TSW and altered seed composition when disrupted in combination with other GTR2 paralogs.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates seed glucosinolate accumulation; single mutant causes moderate reduction and contributes redundantly with other GTR2 homologs.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA06.GTR2 is one of four edited GTR2 homologs contributing to seed glucosinolate accumulation. 2. Its single mutant caused a moderate SGC decline relative to wild type. 3. Combined mutants revealed partial functional redundancy among paralogs.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRT/PTR family transporter; major facilitator superfamily conserved domain
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for multiplex engineering of seed glucosinolate reduction, with caution due to pleiotropy in stacked mutants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to the first GTR2 homolog group with higher similarity to Arabidopsis GTR2.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to negative seed-trait effects in stacked mutants.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates seed glucosinolate accumulation; single mutant causes moderate reduction and contributes redundantly with other GTR2 homologs.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC03.GTR2 is one of four edited GTR2 homologs contributing to seed glucosinolate accumulation. 2. Its single mutant caused a moderate SGC decline relative to wild type. 3. Combined mutants revealed partial functional redundancy among paralogs.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRT/PTR family transporter; major facilitator superfamily conserved domain
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for multiplex engineering of seed glucosinolate reduction, with caution due to pleiotropy in stacked mutants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to the first GTR2 homolog group with higher similarity to Arabidopsis GTR2.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to negative seed-trait effects in stacked mutants.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted candidate gene underlying qGSL.A09.2 contributing to reduced seed glucosinolate content during breeding selection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/QTL mapping; candidate prioritization; haplotype/selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA09.PYRD was predicted as the candidate gene underlying qGSL.A09.2. 2. qGSL.A09.2 had a major effect on seed glucosinolate content and was strongly selected in low-SGC breeding. 3. This locus was part of the co-selected major low-SGC QTL set.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding locus for low seed glucosinolate, especially in combination with GTR2 and HAG1/MYB28 loci.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly supports seed glucosinolate accumulation via associated metabolic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional causality at biochemical step not directly validated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolites; anti-nutritional in rapeseed meal

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted candidate gene underlying qGSL.C09.1 contributing to high seed glucosinolate accumulation; low-SGC haplotype selected during breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/QTL mapping; TWAS association; candidate prioritization; haplotype/selection analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20 and 40 DAF seed development; breeding selection context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC09.HAG1/MYB28 was predicted as the candidate gene underlying qGSL.C09.1. 2. qGSL.C09.1 had major effects on seed glucosinolate content in subpopulations and was strongly selected during low-SGC breeding. 3. HAG1/MYB28 orthologs were positively associated with seed glucosinolate content in TWAS and formed part of the co-selected low-SGC QTL combination.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major breeding target/locus for reducing seed glucosinolate, especially in combination with GTR2 and PYRD loci.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator locus associated with a large-effect seed glucosinolate QTL selected in breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolites; anti-nutritional in rapeseed meal
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct regulatory targets were not experimentally validated in this paper, although MYB28 is a known glucosinolate biosynthesis regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively associated with seed glucosinolate content at 20 and/or 40 DAF
